---
title: "Samuel Schapiro"
description-meta: "Quarto Academic Website Template adapted by Dr. Gang He"
image: "files/images/new_headshot.jpg"
page-navigation: false
toc: false
#sidebar: false
page-layout: full
include-after-body:
  text: |
    <canvas id="bg-network" aria-hidden="true"></canvas>
    <script>
    (function() {
      document.body.classList.add('home-page');
      if (window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches) return;

      const canvas = document.getElementById('bg-network');
      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
      let W, H, dpr, particles = [];
      const N = 55;
      const LINK_DIST = 150;

      function resize() {
        dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
        W = window.innerWidth;
        H = window.innerHeight;
        canvas.width = W * dpr;
        canvas.height = H * dpr;
        canvas.style.width = W + 'px';
        canvas.style.height = H + 'px';
        ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0);
      }
      window.addEventListener('resize', resize);
      resize();

      for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        particles.push({
          x: Math.random() * W,
          y: Math.random() * H,
          vx: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.25,
          vy: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.25,
          r: 1.2 + Math.random() * 1.0,
        });
      }

      function tick() {
        ctx.clearRect(0, 0, W, H);

        for (const p of particles) {
          p.x += p.vx;
          p.y += p.vy;
          if (p.x < 0 || p.x > W) p.vx *= -1;
          if (p.y < 0 || p.y > H) p.vy *= -1;
        }

        ctx.lineWidth = 0.7;
        for (let i = 0; i < particles.length; i++) {
          for (let j = i + 1; j < particles.length; j++) {
            const dx = particles[i].x - particles[j].x;
            const dy = particles[i].y - particles[j].y;
            const d = Math.hypot(dx, dy);
            if (d < LINK_DIST) {
              ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(30, 58, 138, ' + (0.18 * (1 - d / LINK_DIST)).toFixed(3) + ')';
              ctx.beginPath();
              ctx.moveTo(particles[i].x, particles[i].y);
              ctx.lineTo(particles[j].x, particles[j].y);
              ctx.stroke();
            }
          }
        }

        ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(30, 58, 138, 0.55)';
        for (const p of particles) {
          ctx.beginPath();
          ctx.arc(p.x, p.y, p.r, 0, Math.PI * 2);
          ctx.fill();
        }

        requestAnimationFrame(tick);
      }
      tick();
    })();
    </script>


about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-width: 70%
  links:
    # - icon: sun
    #   text: Group
    #   href: https://spiralworks.ai
    #   target: _blank
    - icon: globe
      text: Bio
      href: https://simons.berkeley.edu/people/sam-schapiro
      target: _blank
    - icon: envelope
      text: E-mail
      href: mailto:sam@priorcomputers.ai
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/samuel-schapiro
      target: _blank
    # - icon: youtube
    #   text: YouTube
    #   href: https://www.youtube.com     
    #   target: _blank 
    # - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"   # use academic icons, "" are needed, you also need to install the fontawesome extension
    #   href: https://orcid.org
    #   target: _blank
    # - text: "{{< ai clarivate >}} Web of Science"
    #   href: https://www.webofscience.com  
    #   target: _blank
    # - text: "{{< ai scopus >}} Scopus"
    #   href: https://www.scopus.com
    #   target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=B2UNU6EAAAAJ&hl=en
      target: _blank
#    - text: "{{< ai researchgate >}} Research Gate"
#      href: https://www.researchgate.net
#      target: _blank

listing:   # Comment the listing section and delete corresponding lines in the index.qmd file to remove the recent posts
  - id: recent-posts
    contents: 
      - posts
      - posts/post-links.yml
    exclude:
      title: ""
    type: grid
    max-items: 3
#    image-height: 150px
    page-size: 3 #set same as max-times to remove the page number setting
    fields: [image, date, title, subtitle]
    max-description-length: 75
    sort: 
      - "date desc"
      - "title asc"
---

:::{#hero-heading}

### Currently
Founding research scientist at a stealth AI company.

## Bio
I am an [interdisciplinary scholar](bookshelf.html) studying the science of intelligence. I am currently interested in 

- Uncovering the **fundamental, scientific principles of creativity** governing minds and machines 
- Developing **AI methods** and systems that exhibit creative behavior

Previously, I was a researcher at the [Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing, Berkeley](https://simons.berkeley.edu/homepage). I did my undergrad at the [University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign](https://siebelschool.illinois.edu/), where I studied theoretical computer science.



## Research

### Foundations of Creativity
**Goal:** To uncover fundamental principles of creativity governing minds and machines. Achieved through a combination of conceptual and theoretical work [1], and empirical study of LLMs [2, 3].

#### Recent Papers

1. **S. Schapiro**, J. Black, L. R. Varshney, [Transformational Creativity in Science: A Graphical Theory](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.18687), in the Proceedings of the 16th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 2025. **[Best Short Paper Award]**
2. **S. Schapiro**, A. Gladstone, J. Black, H. Ji. [Do Semantic Distance Tests _Actually_ Predict Creativity in LLMs?](files/SD_NIPS.pdf), Under Review at the 43rd International Conference on Machine Learning, 2026.
3. **S. Schapiro**, S. Shashidhar, A. Gladstone, J. Black, R. Moon, D. Hakkani-Tur, L. R. Varshney, [Combinatorial Creativity: A New Frontier in Generalization Abilities](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.21043), Under Review at 16th International Conference on Learning Representations, 2026.

### Creative AI Systems
**Goal:** To develop methods that make AI models more creative [4], and to apply these techniques to scientific discovery [5].

#### Recent Papers

4. **S. Schapiro**, C. Francisco Park, F. Sosa, L. R. Varshney, [CreativityNeuro: Steering Large Language Weights to Improve Divergent Thinking](files/CN.pdf), Under Review at Third Conference on Language Modeling, 2026.
5. A. Sanyal, **S. Schapiro**, S. Shashidhar, R. Moon, L. R. Varshney, D. Hakkani-Tur, [Spark: A System for Scientifically Creative Idea Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.20090), in the Proceedings of the 16th International Conference on Computational Creativity, 2025.

## Service
- Reviewer at ICML 2026, COLM 2026, NeurIPS 2026, NeurIPS 2024 (Workshop on Creativity & Generative AI)

:::


<!-- Delete the following section and delete corresponding lines in the yaml part to remove the recent posts -->
## Recent Posts
Check out the latest &nbsp;[Papers](posts.qmd#category=paper)&nbsp;, &nbsp;[News](posts.qmd#category=news)&nbsp;, &nbsp;[Events](posts.qmd#category=event)&nbsp;, and &nbsp;[More »](/posts.qmd)

:::{#recent-posts}
:::

<p align="right">[All Posts »](/posts.qmd)</p>
